openFindings.title: Migrated spec uses relative skill-advisor doc paths that resolve to non-existent files
openFindings.impact: Core spec and checked evidence links cannot be followed from the current packet location, so future auditors and implementers are routed to absent files.
openFindings.recommendation: Replace migrated relative paths with current repo-root paths under .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/skill-advisor/ or use packet-stable absolute/repo-root anchors.

openFindings.title: README does not present hook invocation as the primary quick-start path
openFindings.impact: REQ-001 and T005 are marked complete, but the live README quick start leads with native MCP tools and only labels Python as fallback; there is no dedicated hook-invocation primary section.
openFindings.recommendation: Add the promised hook-primary quick-start section with per-runtime registration and keep native tools/Python as operator and fallback paths.

openFindings.title: Feature catalog lacks the claimed Phase 020 hook-surface entries
openFindings.impact: The packet claims a dedicated HOOK SURFACE category with 12 entries, but the live catalog is a Phase 027 eight-group inventory and does not include HMAC cache, AdvisorHookDiagnosticRecord, advisor-hook-health, or privacy-contract catalog entries.
openFindings.recommendation: Add or restore the missing Phase 020 hook-surface catalog entries and update counts/table-of-contents accordingly.

openFindings.title: Manual hook-routing smoke playbook is marked complete but absent
openFindings.impact: REQ-003 and CHK-013 cannot be replayed: the claimed HR-001 through HR-006 smoke playbook is not present in the current package.
openFindings.recommendation: Create the hook-routing playbook or revise completion evidence to point to the actual existing CLI hook/plugin scenarios if they satisfy the requirement.

openFindings.title: Code-alignment audit target paths no longer exist
openFindings.impact: The TS audit ledger is not replayable from the packet as written, and future remediation could inspect the wrong source tree.
openFindings.recommendation: Update scoped TS paths and audit ledger entries to the current skill-advisor/lib layout, then rerun the exact grep/typecheck evidence against those paths.

openFindings.title: Spec status and continuity metadata are stale after completion
openFindings.impact: Resume surfaces may show 'Spec Ready' and 'Dispatch cli-codex' even though graph metadata and implementation summary say complete.
openFindings.recommendation: Refresh spec frontmatter continuity/status fields or document that graph-metadata is the authoritative post-migration status.

openFindings.title: Migrated packet still mixes 022/Phase 022 labels with local 005 path
openFindings.impact: The alias history is valid, but unqualified '022 spec folder' wording makes the current packet harder to identify and increases copy/paste risk.
openFindings.recommendation: Keep migration aliases in metadata, but update human-facing current-path labels to 005-skill-advisor-docs-and-code-alignment.

openFindings.title: Prompt-cache HMAC default secret uses Math.random entropy
openFindings.impact: The cache is in-memory and prompt text is not persisted, so this is not a release blocker. Still, an HMAC key meant to make prompt-derived cache keys opaque should use cryptographic randomness.
openFindings.recommendation: Use crypto.randomBytes for the default per-process HMAC secret.
